hawkish_phrases: labor market had already reached full employment and was projected to tighten further
a delay in gradually removing policy accommodation could result in an overshooting of the Committee's inflation objective
easing in financial conditions that had developed since the Committee's policy normalization process was initiated
the stance of monetary policy remains accommodative
Committee expects to begin implementing its balance sheet normalization program relatively soon
dovish_phrases: inflation on a 12-month basis had declined and were running below 2 percent
some participants expressed concern about the recent decline in inflation
the Committee could afford to be patient under current circumstances in deciding when to increase the federal funds rate further
several indicated that the risks to the inflation outlook could be tilted to the downside
the Committee is monitoring inflation developments closely
summary: The Committee unanimously voted to hold the federal funds rate at 1 to 1-1/4 percent with a mildly hawkish bias, signaling balance sheet normalization would begin relatively soon while expressing concern about below-target inflation and monitoring it closely.
